text: Taqwa (cultivating piety) is seen as a central attribute of a Muslim, a faith that then develops into moral characteristics. According to the Quran taqwa is seen as the believer’s attitude towards Allah; their “heart attitude” toward Allah characterised by obedience to Allah’s commandments and shunning Allah’s prohibitions. This study aims at knowing how the attitude of taqwa can be actualised based on the Islamic exegesis from Buya Hamka’s works entitled Tafsir al-Azhar. Based on the qualitative descriptive library research, this study made an in-depth analysis based on Hamka’s exposition of Quranic verse in Tafsir al-Azhar and was complemented and reinforced by additional readings from other religious texts, journals and academic studies. The study revealed that for Buya Hamka taqwa refers to the spirit of worship; fulfilling the obligatory deeds to increase their mutual relationship with Allah as well as disseminating the spirits of brotherhood and humanity. Hence, based on the theory of Hamka it can be concluded that the realisation of taqwa is consists of two parts of obligations namely vertical obligation or hablun min Allah which is the believer’s obligations in establishing relation towards Allah; and the horizontal obligations or hablun min al-nas which is the believers obligations in socialising or interacting each other as human being.
